question.text: Which of the following tax-saving instruments has the shortest lock-in period?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** ELSS has a lock-in of just 3 years, which is the shortest among all Section 80C tax-saving instruments. Tax saving FD has 5 years, PPF has 15 years, and NPS Tier 1 is locked in till age 60. **The Trap:** Tax saving bank FD may seem like it has a short lock-in because FDs are generally considered liquid instruments, but the tax-saving variant mandates a full 5-year lock-in with no premature withdrawal. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund Distributors) tests ELSS lock-in details; NISM Series X-A (Investment Adviser Level 1) covers all 80C instruments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ELSS's 3-year lock-in is a key selling point for clients who want tax savings with the shortest commitment -- but always explain that equity risk means the value could be lower at redemption than the amount invested.
